# checkpoints/training_plots — Context

## Purpose
Static PNG plots of **training and validation loss/MAE curves** saved at the end of each training run. Useful for quick visual inspection without launching TensorBoard.

## File Layout (per run)

```
training_plots/{run_id}/
├── {run_id}_loss.png    # MSE loss: train vs validation per epoch
└── {run_id}_mae.png     # MAE: train vs validation per epoch
```

## Generated By
`src/train_and_evaluate/train_and_evaluate.py::plot_training_history()`

## Reading the Plots
- **X-axis**: Epoch number
- **Y-axis**: Loss (MSE) or MAE value
- **Blue line**: Training set
- **Orange line**: Validation set
- Vertical drop in the training curve = learning rate decay (`ReduceLROnPlateau`)
- Training stops early if gap between train and val opens significantly (overfitting guard)

## Recommended Patterns
- Smooth convergence with val_loss matching train_loss → good generalisation
- Large gap (val >> train) → overfitting; consider increasing dropout or reducing model size
- No convergence → learning rate too high or sequence length/features need review
